questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Consider a parallel plate capacitor which is maintained at potential $200 \mathrm{~V}$. The separation distance between the plates of capacitor and the area of plates are $1 \mathrm{~mm}$ and $20 \mathrm{~cm}^2$, respectively. Calculate displacement current in $1 \mu \mathrm{s}$.
questions.solution:
Displacement current,
$\begin{aligned} I_d & =\varepsilon_0 \frac{d \phi}{d t}=\varepsilon_0 \frac{\Delta \phi}{\Delta t} \\ & =\varepsilon_0 \frac{\Delta E A}{\Delta t} \quad(\because \phi=E \cdot A)\end{aligned}$
$=\frac{\varepsilon_0 \cdot \Delta V \cdot A}{\Delta t \cdot d} \quad\left(\because E=\frac{V}{d}\right)$
Here, $\Delta V=200 \mathrm{~V}$,
$\begin{aligned} \Delta t & =1 \mu \mathrm{s}=10^{-6} \mathrm{~s}, \\ d & =1 \mathrm{~mm}=1 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~m} \\ \text { and } \quad A & =20 \mathrm{~cm}^2=20 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{~m}^2\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} \therefore \quad I_d & =\frac{8.85 \times 10^{-12} \times 200 \times 20 \times 10^{-4}}{10^{-6} \times 10^{-3}} \\ & =3.5 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~A}=3.5 \mathrm{~mA}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A laser beam has intensity $2.1 \times 10^{15} \mathrm{~W} / \mathrm{m}^2$. The amplitude of magnetic field in the beam in approximately is
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{I}=\frac{\mathrm{B}_0^2}{2 \mu_0} \mathrm{C}$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad 2.1 \times 10^{15}=\frac{\mathrm{B}_0^2 \times 3 \times 10^8}{8 \pi \times 10^{-7}} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{B}_0^2=17.64 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{B}_0=4.2 \mathrm{~T}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In compton scattering process, the incident $X$-radiation is scattered at an angle $60^{\circ}$. The wavelength of the scattered radiation is $0.22 Å$. The wavelength of the incident $X$-radiation in $Å$ units is
questions.solution:
For Compton effect we have the relation
$\lambda_2-\lambda_1=\frac{h}{m_0 c}(1-\cos \theta)$
$\lambda_1=$ wavelength of incident radiation
$\lambda_2=$ wavelength of scattered radiation
$\begin{aligned}
\therefore 0.22-\lambda_1 & =0.024(1-\cos 60) \\
0.22-\lambda_1 & =0.024\left(1-\frac{1}{2}\right) \\
0.22-\lambda_1 & =0.024 \times \frac{1}{2} \\
\lambda_1 & =0.22-0.012=0.208 Å
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
A laser beam of power 27 mW has a cross-sectional area of 10 mm2. The magnitude of the maximum electric field in this electromagnetic wave is given by

[Given permittivity of space ε0≈9×10-12 C2 N-1 m-2, speed of light c=3×108 m s-1
questions.solution:
I=PA=12ε0E02c

∴E0=2Pε0CA=2×27×10-3×36π×1093×108×10×10-6

E0=1.4 kV m-1
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The radiation energy emitted per second by a point source is $100 \mathrm{~W}$. If the efficiency of the source is $4 \%$, then the rms value of the electric field at distance of $2 \mathrm{~m}$ is [use $\frac{1}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0}=9 \times 10^9$ in SI unit]
questions.solution:
where, $P_{\text {avg }}=$ average power generated by source $=4 \% \text { of } 100 \mathrm{~W}=\frac{4}{100} \times 100 \mathrm{~W}=4 \mathrm{~W}$ $A=$ area to which energy is transmitted $=4 \pi r^2=4 \pi \times(2)^2=4 \pi \times 4=16 \pi \mathrm{m}^2$ On putting the values into Eq. (i), we have
where, $\varepsilon_0=$ permittivity of free space and $E_{\mathrm{rms}}=$ root mean square $(\mathrm{rms})$ value of electric field. $c=$ speed of light in vacuum $=3 \times 10^8 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ On putting the values into Eq. (iii), we get $I_{\mathrm{avg}}=\varepsilon_0 \times E_{\mathrm{rms}}^2 \times 3 \times 10^8$ On equating both the Eqs. (ii) and (iv), we get $\begin{gathered} \frac{1}{4 \pi}=\varepsilon_0 \times E_{\mathrm{rms}}^2 \times 3 \times 10^8 \Rightarrow \frac{1}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0 \times 3 \times 10^8}=E_{\mathrm{rms}}^2 \\ E_{\mathrm{rms}}^2=\frac{1}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0} \times \frac{1}{3 \times 10^8}=9 \times 10^9 \times \frac{1}{3 \times 10^8} \\ \quad\left(\because \frac{1}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0}=9 \times 10^9 \mathrm{Nm}^2 / \mathrm{C}^2\right) \\ =3 \times 10=\sqrt{30} \mathrm{~V} / \mathrm{m} \end{gathered}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The correct statement among the following is
options: Electromagnetic waves cannot travel in vacuum
Electromagnetic waves are produced by charges moving with uniform velocity
Electromagnetic waves carry both energy and momentum as they propagate through space.
questions.solution:
(a) is incorrect because EM wave doesn't require any medium to travel thus they can travel in vacuum.
(b) is incorrect because EM waves are transverse waves.
(c) is incorrect because EM waves are produced by an accelerating charge.
(d) is correct as EM waves carry both energy and momentum.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Electromagnetic waves travel in a medium with a speed of <math> <mrow> <mtext>2</mtext><mo>×</mo><msup> <mrow> <mtext>10</mtext></mrow> <mn>8</mn> </msup> <mtext> </mtext><mtext>m</mtext><mtext> </mtext><msup> <mtext>s</mtext> <mrow> <mo>−</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow> </msup> <mo>.</mo></mrow> </math> The relative permeability of the medium is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn></math>. Find the relative permittivity.
questions.solution:
Here, v=2× 10 8  m  s −1 and μ r =1

The speed of electromagnetic waves in a medium is given by

v = 1 μ ε , ....(i)

where μ and ε are absolute permeability and absolute permittivity of the medium.

Now, μ= μ 0    μ r and ε= ε 0   ε r

Therefore, the equation (i) becomes

v = 1 μ 0 μ r × ε 0 ε r = 1 μ 0 ε 0 × 1 μ r ε r = c μ r ε r

or ε r = c 2 v 2 μ r = 3 × 10 8 2 2 × 10 8 2 × 1 = 2.25
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: Assume that a lamp radiates power <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi></math> uniformly in all directions. The magnitude of electric field strength at a distance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">r</mi></math> from the lamp is
questions.solution:
Intensity, I=powerarea=P4πr2 = average energy density × velocity=12ε0E02c

∴E0=2P4πε0r2c=P2πε0r2c

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: X-rays of wavelength $0.140 \mathrm{~nm}$ are scattered from a block of carbon. What will be the wavelengths of $X$-rays scattered at $90^{\circ}$ ?
questions.solution:
For $\phi=90^{\circ}, \cos \phi=0$
So,
$\begin{aligned}
& \lambda^{\prime}=\lambda+\frac{h}{m_e c} \\
& =0.140 \times 10^{-9}+\frac{6.63 \times 10^{-34}}{\left(9.1 \times 10^{-31}\right)\left(3 \times 10^8\right)} \\
& =\left(0.140 \times 10^{-9}+2.4 \times 10^{-12}\right) \mathrm{m} \\
& =0.142 \mathrm{~nm}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn><mi> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">W</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi></math>energy density of sunlight is incident normally on the surface of a solar panel. Some part of incident energy (<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>25</mn><mo>%</mo></math>) is reflected from the surface and the rest is absorbed. The force exerted on <math><mn>1</mn><mi> </mi><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math> surface area will be close to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>c</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>8</mn></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced></math>
questions.solution:
The pressure exerted by the photons

 P=0.75×Ic +0.25×2Ic     ( P = pressure, c = speed of light, I = intensity )

P=1.25 ×Ic

 We know the force can be computed using

F=P×A   ( F = force, P = pressure, A = area )

F=1.25×50×13×108

F=20.83×10-8 N
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">W</mi></math> electric bulb produces electromagnetic radiation with electric field amplitude of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> at a distance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math>. Assuming it as a point source, estimate the efficiency of the bulb.
questions.solution:
Intensity =power×ηA     ...1

We know,

12I=12ε0E2c

I=8.854×10-12223×108

I=0.0106 w m-2        ...2

On substituting 2 in 1, 

0.0106=100×η4×π×102

⇒ η=0.133=13.3%
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
The magnetic field in a plane electromagnetic wave is given as
$\mathbf{B}=\left(3 \times 10^{-7} \mathrm{~T}\right) \sin \left(3 \times 10^4 x+9 \times 10^{12} t\right) \hat{j}$
The electric field of this wave is given as
questions.solution:
Given, Magnetic field in a plane EM wave,
$B=\left(3 \times 10^{-7} \mathrm{~T}\right) \sin \left(3 \times 10^4 x+9 \times 10^{12} t\right) \mathrm{T}$
Here, $B_0=3 \times 10^{-7} \mathrm{~T}$
Amplitude of electric field,
$\begin{aligned} E_0 & =B_0 c \\ & =3 \times 10^{-7} \times 3 \times 10^8\end{aligned}$
$\Rightarrow \quad E_0=90 \mathrm{Vm}^{-1}$
From the given equation of $B$, it is clear that $E M$ wave is travelling along negative direction of
$X$ - axis i.e.
$\therefore$ If $\hat{\mathbf{n}}$ be the unit vector along the direction of electric field, then
$\hat{\mathbf{n}} \times \hat{j}=-\hat{i}$
Clearly, $\hat{\mathbf{n}}=\hat{k}$
Hence, $E=E_0 \sin (k x+\omega t) \hat{k}$
$=90 \sin \left(3 \times 10^4 x+9 \times 10^{12} t\right) \hat{k} \mathrm{Vm}^{-1}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Radio waves are in the frequency range of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo> </mo><mi>kHz</mi><mo> </mo><mi>to</mi><mo> </mo><mn>300</mn><mo> </mo><mi>GHz</mi></math>. In the frequency spectrum of electromagnetic waves these waves are:
questions.solution: Radio waves are in the lowest frequency range of the electromagnetic spectrum. Above that, we have infrared, visible, ultraviolet, X-rays and gamma rays. Like all other electromagnetic waves, radio waves also travel with the speed of light. The wavelength of radio waves is in the range of 1 mm to 100 km. Naturally occurring radio waves are produced by lightning or by astronomical objects. Artificially generated radio waves are used for communication.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Modified Ampere's circuital law stated by Maxwell is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi>I</mi><mo>=</mo></math> conduction current, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">ϵ</mi><mn>0</mn></msub><mfrac><mrow><mi>d</mi><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">ϕ</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">E</mi></msub></mrow><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi></mrow></mfrac><mo>=</mo></math> displacement current, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">μ</mi><mn>0</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">ε</mi><mn>0</mn></msub></math> is permeability and permittivity of free space)
questions.solution:
The total current through the circuit will be the sum of the conduction current and the displacement current. Ampere's law has only conduction current but after Maxwell modification, displacement current is also added in it. The Maxwell-Ampere equation is given by,

∮B→·dl→=μ0Itotal

∮B→·dl→=μ0I+ε0·dϕEdt.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Let $\mathbf{E}_0$ and $\mathbf{B}_0$ denote the amplitude of electric and magnetic field of a plane electromagnetic wave in air. The magnitude of the average momentum transferred per unit area and per unit time to a totally absorbing surface is
questions.solution:
Intensity of electromagnetic wave,
$$
I=\frac{1}{2} \varepsilon_0 E_0^2 c=\frac{1}{2} \frac{B_0^2 c}{\mu_0}
$$
where, $B_0=$ electric field and $c=$ speed of light.
Now, momentum transfer per unit area per unit time, which is also called radiation pressure.
$$
\begin{gathered}
\frac{1}{A}\left(\frac{\Delta p}{\Delta t}\right)=\frac{I}{c}=\frac{\frac{1}{2} \varepsilon_0 E_0^2 c}{c} \\
\frac{I}{c}=\frac{1}{2} \varepsilon_0 E_0^2
\end{gathered}
$$
[where, $\Delta p=$ momentum transferred, $\Delta t=$ time and $A=$ area.]
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A large parallel plate capacitor, whose plates have an area of <math><mn>1</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math> and are separated from each other by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math>, is being charged at a rate of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>25</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. If the dielectric between the plates has the dielectric constant <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn></math>, then the displacement current at this instant is
questions.solution:
The capacity of a parallel plate capacitor is 
C=ε0KAd=(8.85×10-12)×10×110-3
=8.85×10-8 F
The displacement current is
i=ddtCV=CdVdt=8.85×10-8×25
i=2.2×10-6 A.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Calculate the wavelength of the $k_a$ line for $z=31$, when $a=5 \times 10^7 \mathrm{~Hz}^{1 / 2}$ for a characteristic X-ray spectrum.
questions.solution:
Given, $z=31$ and $a=5 \times 10^7 \mathrm{~Hz}^{1 / 2}$
$\begin{aligned}
\sqrt{v} & =a(z-1) \\
\text { or } \quad v & =a^2(z-1)^2 \\
& =\left(5 \times 10^7\right)^2(31-1)^2 \\
& =25 \times 10^{14} \times 30 \times 30 \\
& =2.25 \times 10^{18} \mathrm{~Hz} \\
\therefore \quad & =\frac{c}{\lambda} \Rightarrow \lambda=\frac{c}{v} \\
& =\frac{3 \times 10^8 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}}{2.25 \times 10^{18} \mathrm{~Hz}} \\
& =1.33 \times 10^{-10} \mathrm{~m} \\
& =1.33 Å
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: An electromagnetic wave of frequency \(\mathrm{I} \times 10^{14} \mathrm{~Hz}\) is propagating along \(\mathrm{z}\)-axis. The amplitude of electric field is \(4 \mathrm{Vm}^{-1}\), then energy density of the electric field will be (Permittivity of free space \(=8.8 \times 10^{-12} \mathrm{C}^2 \mathrm{~N}^{-1} \mathrm{~m}^{-2}\) )
questions.solution:
Given, electromagnetic wave frequency, \(f_m=1.0 \times 10^{14} \mathrm{~Hz}\) 
amplitude of the electric field, \(E_0=4 \mathrm{Vm}^{-1}\) 
permittivity of free space, \(\varepsilon_0=8.8 \times 10^{-12} \mathrm{C}^2 \mathrm{~N}^{-1} \mathrm{~m}^{-2}\) 
The value of energy density (energy/volume) is given by
\(\therefore \quad u=\frac{1}{2} \varepsilon_0 E_0^2\)
Putting the given values, we get
\(\begin{aligned}
 =\frac{1}{2} \times 8.8 \times 10^{-12} \times(4)^2 \mathrm{~J} / \mathrm{m}^3  =\frac{1}{2} \times 8.8 \times 16 \times 10^{-12} \\
 \therefore \quad u  =70.4 \times 10^{-12} \mathrm{Jm}^{-3}
\end{aligned}\)
Hence, the energy density of the electric field will be \(70.4 \times 10^{-12} \mathrm{Jm}^{-3}\).
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A conducting ring of radius 1 m is placed in a uniform magnetic field B, of 0.01 tesla oscillating with frequency 100 Hz with its plane at right angles to B→. What will be the induced electric field?
questions.solution:
In a constant magnetic field, conducting ring oscillates with a frequency of 100 Hz.

i.e., T=1100 s, in time T 2

 

to zero.

⇒  Induced emf = change in flux time

=BAT/2=2BAT=2B×πr2T=2×0.01×π×121/100=4π volts

The induced electric field along the circle, using Maxwell equation, is,∮E→·dl→=-dϕdt=AdBdt=e

⇒   E=12πr×πr2×dBdt=e2πr=4π2πr=2 V m-1
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q:
When two different materials A and B having atomic number Z1 and Z2 are used as the target in Coolidge γ-ray tube at different operating voltage V1 and V2 respectively their spectrums are found as below.



The correct relation is
questions.solution:
λ min=hceV

⇒       λ∝1V

⇒    V1>V2

f=aZ-b Moseley's law

 f ∝Z-12

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: An electromagnetic wave of frequency <math> <mrow> <mn>1</mn><mo>×</mo><msup> <mrow> <mn>10</mn></mrow> <mrow> <mn>14</mn></mrow> </msup> <mtext> </mtext><mtext>Hz</mtext></mrow> </math> is propagating along <math> <mi>z</mi> </math> -axis. The amplitude of the electric field is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mtext> </mtext><mtext>V/m</mtext></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>ε</mi><mn>0</mn></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>8</mn><mtext>.</mtext><mn>8</mn><mo>×</mo><mn>1</mn><msup><mn>0</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><msup><mtext> C</mtext><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>/</mo><msup><mtext>N-m</mtext><mn>2</mn></msup></math>, then the average energy density of electric field will be
questions.solution:
f⁡ = 1 0 1 4  Hz
E 0 =4 V/m
∈ 0 = 8.8 × 1 0 - 1 2 C 2 N - m 2
Energy density of electric field
= 1 2 (Total energy density)
= 1 2 · 1 2 ∈ 0 E 2
= 1 2 . 1 2 8.8 × 1 0 - 1 2 × 4 2
= 1 2 · 1 2 ×16×8.8× 10 −12   J/m 3
=35.2× 10 −12   J/m 3
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: What is the amplitude of the electric field in a parallel beam of light intensity $\left(\frac{15}{\pi}\right) \frac{\mathrm{W}}{\mathrm{m}^2}$ ? $\left[\right.$ Assume,$\left.\frac{1}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0}=9 \times 10^9 \frac{\mathrm{Nm}^2}{\mathrm{C}^2}\right]$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: An electromagnetic wave of frequency $2 \mathrm{MHz}$ propagates from vacuum to a non-magnetic medium of relative permittivity 9 . Then its' wavelength
questions.solution:
In vacuum wavelength,
$$
\lambda_0=\frac{c}{f_0}=\frac{3 \times 10^8}{2 \times 10^6}=1.5 \times 10^2=150 \mathrm{~m}
$$
In medium, speed of wave is
$$
v=\frac{\mathcal{c}}{\sqrt{\varepsilon_r \mu_r}} \approx \frac{\mathcal{c}}{\sqrt{\varepsilon_r}}
$$
(as medium is non-magnetic)
$$
\therefore \quad v=\frac{\mathcal{c}}{\sqrt{9}}=\frac{\mathcal{c}}{3}=1 \times 10^8 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}
$$
Frequency remaining same, wavelength in medium is $\lambda=\frac{v}{f_0}=\frac{10^8}{2 \times 10^6}=50 \mathrm{~m}$
$\therefore$ Wavelength decreases by $100 \mathrm{~m}$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: An electromagnetic wave having equation, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi mathvariant="italic">E</mi><mo mathvariant="italic">→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><msub><mi>E</mi><mn>0</mn></msub><mi>cos</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>k</mi><mi>z</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>ω</mi><mi>t</mi><mo>)</mo><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></math> travels in vacuum along <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>z</mi></math>-direction. A rectangular loop is to be kept such that at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>t</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>, displacement current may flow through its area. Which of the following is the correct orientation?
questions.solution:
We know that electromagnetic waves have electric as well as magnetic vectors which are vibrating perpendicular to each other and these two vectors are also vibrating perpendicular to the direction of the motion.

Here, the direction of displacement current is the same as the direction of motion of wave which is along i^ [x-axis]. So, area vector should also be along i^, i.e., yz plane.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: An electromaghetic radiation has an energy <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>14</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>keV</mi></math>. To which region of the electromagnetic spectrum it belongs
questions.solution:
Recall the formula of energy,

E=hcλ=14.4×1000×1.6×10-19 J⇒hcλ=6.6×10-34×3×108λ=14.4×1000×1.6×10-19 J⇒λ=6.6×10-34×3×10814.4×1000×1.6×10-19 J=0.859×10-10 m⇒λ≈1 A∘ X-rays
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
The energies required to set up in a cube of side 10 cm, (a) A uniform electric field of 107 V m-1 and (b) A uniform magnetic field if 0.25 Wb m-2, are respectively about
μ0=4π×10-7 H m-1,ϵ0=8.9×10-12 F m-1
questions.solution:
Recall the formula of energy in terms of electric field and magnetic field strength,

EE=12ε0E2V & EB=12ε0Bc2V⇒EE=12ε0E2V ⇒EE=12×8.9×10-12×1072×10-3=0.445 J⇒EB=12ε0Bc2V⇒EB=12×8.9×10-12×0.252×3×1082×10-3=25 J
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The electric field for an electromangetic wave in free space is $\mathbf{E}=\mathbf{i} 30 \cos \left(k z-5 \times 10^8 t\right)$, where magnitude of $E$ is in $\mathrm{V} / \mathrm{m}$. The magnitude of wave vector, $k$ is (velocity of em wave in free space $=3 \times 10^8 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ )
questions.solution:
The given
$\mathbf{E}=\mathbf{i} 30 \cos \left(k z-5 \times 10^8 t\right)$
We know that,
$\mathbf{E}=\mathrm{iV} \cos (k z-\omega t)$
Comparing the both equations,
$\omega=5 \times 10^8 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}$
But we know that also
$K=\frac{2 \pi}{\lambda} \text { and } \omega=2 \pi \nu$
where, $\lambda$ is wavelength and $v$ is frequency of the wave
$\begin{array}{ll}
\therefore & \frac{\omega}{k}=\frac{2 \pi v}{2 \pi / \lambda}=v \lambda=C \\
\Rightarrow & C=\frac{\omega}{k} \\
\text {or } & k=\frac{\omega}{C}=\frac{5 \times 10^8}{3 \times 10^8} \\
\Rightarrow & k=\frac{5}{3}=1.66 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{m}
\end{array}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
